overall_summary: ElizaOS is transitioning toward a v2.0 beta launch with a focus on automation plugins and decentralized identity, while the community manages ongoing security challenges from scam bots and the closure of token migration.
key_facts: Token migration from ai16z to elizaos is officially closed and no longer available to users.
ElizaBAO is developing a Milady project integration for ElizaOS v2.0 featuring a Polymarket plugin.
A major database refactor (PR #6509) and the SAID Protocol for on-chain Solana identity (PR #6510) were initiated.
Users reported that plugins including plugin-linear and plugin-memory are broken in ElizaOS version 1.7.2.
A dedicated VPS orchestration system named 'zeitgaist' was released, integrating Conway terminals and OpenClaw.
The 1.x task system and v2.0.0 built-in autonomous system were identified as distinct from the plugin-autonomous implementation.
A new credit-building plugin with certified mail capabilities was introduced by Meme Broker.
Moderators confirmed that scam bots are persistently targeting new users in the Discord discussion channel.
open_questions: Should I use ElizaOS 'v2-develop' instead of 'alpha' channel for production implementations?
For autonomous cron-like behavior, do you rely on some plugin, ElizaOS 2.0 autonomy, or did you code something separate?
How do you handle FCRA compliance verification before sending in the credit-builder plugin?
How can I get started working with the project? (addressed to MochinoLabs but preempted by scammers)

new_issues_prs.title: Major database refactor
new_issues_prs.author: unknown
new_issues_prs.significance: Improves long-term system architecture and performance.

new_issues_prs.title: SAID Protocol integration for on-chain identity
new_issues_prs.author: unknown
new_issues_prs.significance: Enables verifiable agent signatures on Solana.

overall_focus.claim: Focus on stabilizing the core framework for beta launch, including clearing functional blockers and expanding messaging integrations for WhatsApp and Gmail.

discord_updates.summary: Discussions centered on the end of token migration, reporting broken plugins in v1.7.2, and warnings against pervasive scam bots targeting newcomers.
Introduction of a high-level AI systems developer specializing in healthcare and finance fraud detection; presentation of the zeitgaist VPS orchestration project.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Developers expressed frustration with breaking changes in alpha releases and the lack of visibility for community-contributed infrastructure projects like zeitgaist.

strategic_insights.insight: The presence of three different autonomous implementations (v2.0 builtin, plugin-autonomous, and Milady/OpenClaw) suggests a need for architectural convergence.
implications_or_questions: Which autonomy standard will be officially supported in the v2.0 stable release?

strategic_insights.insight: Transitioning from social-only agents to regulated industry tools (FCRA compliance, certified mail) increases the legal risk profile for the ecosystem.
implications_or_questions: Does the framework need a standardized compliance-checking layer for sensitive plugins?

market_analysis.observation: ElizaOS is expanding into prediction markets via Polymarket and Verifiable Oracle Thesis integrations.
market_analysis.relevance: Diversifies the utility of ElizaOS agents into financial and forecasting sectors.
